summary: Cervical dilation measurements begin when it reaches about 6 cm during active labor; thus, paographs start here to accurately monitor pregnancy progression. Agent1 reasoned that since cervical dilation typically starts significantly changing from an inactive state near or after 6 centimeters due to entering the active stage of labor, measuring begins then using tools like paographs for precise follow-up care throughout delivery stages. This ensures accurate documentation aligning well with standard medical practices regarding obstetric management milestones.
